decisions.decision: Keep the existing shared `OrderResponse` stable for ProbabilityEdit callers and add a dedicated EventTrade success type in `frontend/src/lib/api/types.ts` so this step fixes the stale EventTrade surface without reopening unrelated mutation typing.
alternatives_rejected: Broaden the shared `OrderResponse` for every mutation in this step.
Keep EventTrade on the stale generic order model that omits persisted trade fields like `size`, `side`, `price`, and `notional`.

decisions.decision: Add an additive `AccountExposureResponse` that mirrors the backend `{ account, meta }` contract exactly, including `maxPositionSize`, `updatedAt`, array-based `positions`, and nullable `lastOrderId`/`lastCommandId` fields on each position row.
alternatives_rejected: Fold exposure fields into `AccountRiskResponse` or reuse the `/risk` model.
Invent a looser exposure type that hides backend nullability or changes the response structure.

decisions.decision: Make `size` the canonical public EventTrade request field and model the specialized accepted-order response around persisted backend fields only, leaving the backend-only legacy `amount` alias and preview-only netting fields out of the frontend public type surface.
alternatives_rejected: Expose both `size` and deprecated `amount` in the public payload type.
Pull preview-only `currentNetSize` or `resultingNetSize` fields into the accepted EventTrade success type.

steps.task: Patch `frontend/src/lib/api/types.ts` to introduce the exposure support interfaces and `AccountExposureResponse` next to the existing account risk types, matching backend field names, nesting, and nullability exactly.

Refine the EventTrade request and response types in the same file by adding canonical `EventTradePayload.size` and introducing a dedicated EventTrade mutation success type whose `order` shape includes the persisted backend trade fields while keeping `OrderResponse` unchanged for ProbabilityEdit.

Sanity-check the resulting type exports against current frontend consumers so the new models remain additive, downstream client updates can swap EventTrade to the specialized response cleanly, and no probability-edit typing regresses.
